popularStory.headline: Monument in St. Mary's Church
popularStory.description:
A monument to Lord Edward Seymour survives in St Mary's Church, Berry Pomeroy, standing against the north wall of the north aisle of the Seymour Chapel. 
 The monument consists of three main effigies, …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Edward Seymour於1539年5月22日出生時，他的父親Sir Edward Seymour 1st Duke of Somerset33歲，母親Anne Stanhope30歲。他於1560年12月與Catherine Grey Countess Of Hertford在英國米德萊可倫敦結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。他去世於1621年4月6日在英國哈普爾特利特利 艾比享年81歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭維爾爾。

nameMeanings.name: Seymour
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (London): of Norman origin, a habitational name from Saint-Maur-des-Fossés (Seine), and possibly also from Saint-Maur-des-Bois (Manche) and Saint-Maur-sur-Loire (Touraine), named from the dedication of their churches to Saint Maur (see   Moore   3). The family of this name who have held the title Duke of Somerset since 1547 are said by some to have originated in Saint-Maur-des-Fossés (Seine), while others claim they are from Saint-Maur-sur-Loire (Touraine). They rose to prominence under the Tudors, when Edward Seymour (died 1552) held a high position of office under Henry VIII, who was married to Edward's sister, Jane. The Norman name has been established in Ireland since at least the 17th century.

variants.name: Moore
meanings: English: from Middle English more ‘moor, marsh, fen’ (Old English mōr), hence a topographic name for someone who lived in such a place, or a habitational name from any of various places called with this word, as for example Moore in Cheshire or More in Shropshire.
English (of Norman origin): ethnic name from Old French more ‘Moor’, either someone from North Africa or, more often, a nickname for someone thought to resemble a Moor. Compare Morrell and Moreau .

English (of Norman origin): from the Middle English personal name More (Old French More, Maur, Latin Maurus), originally denoting either ‘Moor’ or someone with a swarthy complexion (compare Morrell , Morrin , Morris , and sense 2 above). There was a 6th-century Christian saint of this name.

variants.name: Semar
meanings: Germanized form of French Saint Marc, the name of a Huguenot family that migrated to the Palatinate.
English: variant of   Seymour  . This surname is very rare in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: Edward
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Edward Seymour was born on 22 May 1539, his father, Sir Edward Seymour 1st Duke of Somerset, was 33 and his mother, Anne Stanhope, was 30. He married Catherine Grey Countess Of Hertford in December 1560, in London, Middlesex, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. He died on 6 April 1621, in Netley Abbey, Netley, Hampshire, England, at the age of 81, and was buried in Wiltshire, England, United Kingdom.

Il a épousé Catherine Grey Countess Of Hertford en décembre 1560, à Londres, Middlesex, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Il est décédé le 6 avril 1621, à Netley Abbey, Netley, Hampshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 81 ans, et a été enterré à Wiltshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Edward Seymour(이)가 1539년 5월 22일에 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Edward Seymour 1st Duke of Somerset(은)는 33세였고, 그의 어머니 Anne Stanhope(은)는 30세였다. 그는 1560년 12월 영국 미들레세스 런던에서 Catherine Grey Countess Of Hertford(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1621년 4월 6일 영국 함프실르 네틀리 네틀리 애브브이에서 81세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 윌츠히르에 묻혔다.

Ele casou-se com Catherine Grey Countess Of Hertford em dezembro de 1560, em London, Middlesex, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Edward Seymour родился 22 мая 1539 г., его отцу по имени Sir Edward Seymour 1st Duke of Somerset было 33 года, а матери, которую звали Anne Stanhope, 30. Он женился на женщине по имени Catherine Grey Countess Of Hertford декабрь 1560 г. в местности Лондон, миддлесекс, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Он умер 6 апреля 1621 г. в местности нетли эбби, нетли, хампшир, Англия в возрасте 81 год и был похоронен в местности уилцхир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Edward Seymour nació el 22 de mayo de 1539, su papá, Sir Edward Seymour 1st Duke of Somerset, tenía 33 años y su mamá, Anne Stanhope, tenía 30 años. Se casó con Catherine Grey Countess Of Hertford en diciembre de 1560, en London, Middlesex, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos. Murió el 6 de abril de 1621, en Netley Abbey, Netley, Hampshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 81 años, y fue sepultado en Wiltshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
